pack_intro: How you work best — energy, deadlines, learning.

questions.question: Important deadline in 3 days. You haven't started. What happens?
questions.why_this_matters: Tells AI whether to break tasks down or let you work your way.

options.label: I thrive on pressure — I'll crush it in one burst
options.export_rule: I work best under pressure. Don't nag about deadlines — help me execute when I'm ready.

options.label: I wait for the pressure, then go hard in one burst — it's my process
options.export_rule: I procrastinate then burst. When I'm in burst mode: break tasks into smallest next steps. No lectures about planning.

options.label: I'd never be in this situation — I plan ahead
options.export_rule: Help me plan ahead. Break projects into milestones with clear deadlines.

options.label: I'd feel overwhelmed and might shut down briefly
options.export_rule: IF I seem overwhelmed THEN give me just ONE tiny next step. Don't show the full list.

questions.question: When does your brain work best?
questions.why_this_matters: Helps AI suggest tasks at the right time.

options.label: Morning person — sharp before noon, fade after lunch
options.export_rule: My peak focus is morning. Suggest demanding tasks before noon.

options.label: Afternoon peak — slow start, hit stride after lunch
options.export_rule: My peak focus is afternoon. Don't front-load mornings with hard tasks.

options.label: Night owl — best work happens after 8 PM
options.export_rule: I'm a night owl. My best work happens late. Don't assume morning productivity.

options.label: Unpredictable — I work in bursts when inspiration hits
options.export_rule: My energy is unpredictable. Suggest 15-25 min tasks I can grab when motivation hits. Don't plan long sessions.

questions.question: You need to learn a completely new tool by next week. Your move?
questions.why_this_matters: Shapes how AI teaches you — examples vs theory vs docs.

options.label: Jump in, break things, figure it out
options.export_rule: I learn by doing. Give me working examples to modify, not theory to read.

options.label: Find the best tutorial or course first
options.export_rule: I learn best with structured tutorials. Give step-by-step guides with explanations.

options.label: Read the docs, understand the model, then try
options.export_rule: Explain the mental model first — how things connect. Then I'll apply it.

options.label: Find someone who knows it and work alongside them
options.export_rule: Walk me through it interactively. Step by step together, not a dump of information.

questions.question: When it matters: getting it RIGHT or getting it FAST?
questions.why_this_matters: Tells AI whether to optimize for speed or quality.

options.label: 80% now beats 100% later. Ship it.
options.export_rule: Optimize for speed. Give me the 80% solution fast. I'll refine if needed.

options.label: If it's worth doing, it's worth doing right
options.export_rule: Prioritize correctness over speed. Point out edge cases and potential issues.

options.label: Depends — prototype fast, production right
options.export_rule: Match quality to context. Prototypes = fast and scrappy. Production = thorough and tested.

questions.question: You're overwhelmed with too much on your plate. What helps?
questions.why_this_matters: Determines how AI helps when you're stressed.

options.label: Give me a list — seeing everything organized calms me down
options.export_rule: When I'm overwhelmed: organize everything into a prioritized list. Seeing order helps.

options.label: Just tell me the ONE smallest thing to do next
options.export_rule: When I'm overwhelmed: give me ONE tiny next step. Not a list. Not a plan. Just the next thing.

options.label: Talk it through with me — help me think out loud
options.export_rule: When I'm stressed: help me think out loud. Ask questions, don't prescribe solutions.

options.label: Pick something for me and get me started — momentum helps
options.export_rule: When I'm stuck: pick the easiest win and start me on it. Momentum breaks paralysis.

questions.question: Do you manage or lead other people?
questions.why_this_matters: Managers need AI that helps them delegate, not do everything themselves.

options.label: Yes — I manage a team or lead projects
options.export_rule: I manage people. Help me delegate, communicate to teams, and think strategically — not just execute.

options.label: No — I work mostly alone or as an individual contributor
options.export_rule: I work solo. Focus on execution and hands-on solutions.

options.label: Both — I lead but also do hands-on work
options.export_rule: I'm a player-coach — I lead AND do hands-on work. Help me balance both.

questions.question: In your main area of work — how experienced are you?
questions.why_this_matters: Determines how much AI should explain vs assume you know.

options.label: Learning — I'm new to this field (< 2 years)
options.export_rule: I'm relatively new to my field. Explain concepts clearly, give examples, don't assume knowledge.

options.label: Competent — I know my way around (2-5 years)
options.export_rule: I have solid experience. Skip basics but explain advanced concepts when relevant.

options.label: Expert — this is my domain (5+ years)
options.export_rule: I'm an expert in my field. Don't explain fundamentals. Challenge me with advanced perspectives.

questions.question: When you're unsure about something, you usually...
questions.why_this_matters: Tells AI how to handle your uncertainty — push you forward or give you space to research.

options.label: Research until I'm confident, then act
options.export_rule: When I'm unsure, give me data and evidence. Help me research before deciding.

options.label: Ask someone I trust for their take
options.export_rule: Give me your honest recommendation when I'm unsure. I value expert opinions.

options.label: Just try it and see what happens
options.export_rule: Don't over-analyze. Help me try things quickly and learn from results.

options.label: Avoid it until I absolutely have to decide
options.export_rule: When I'm avoiding a decision, break it into smaller steps. Don't present the full decision at once.

questions.question: How do you prefer to collaborate?
questions.why_this_matters: Shapes whether AI suggests async or real-time solutions.

options.label: Async — messages, docs, PRs. I hate meetings.
options.export_rule: I prefer async communication. Suggest written solutions, docs, and messages over calls.

options.label: Real-time — calls, pair work, live sessions
options.export_rule: I work best in real-time collaboration. Suggest pairing, calls, and live sessions.

options.label: Mix — async for routine, sync for complex
options.export_rule: Async for routine tasks, real-time for complex problems. Match the medium to the complexity.

options.label: Solo — I work best completely alone
options.export_rule: I work solo. Don't suggest collaboration tools or team approaches unless I ask.

questions.question: How many things do you work on at once?
questions.why_this_matters: Determines whether AI should help you focus or help you juggle.

options.label: One thing at a time — deep focus
options.export_rule: I do one thing at a time. Don't pile on suggestions. Help me go deep on the current task.

options.label: 2-3 things in rotation
options.export_rule: I rotate between 2-3 projects. Help me context-switch efficiently.

options.label: Everything at once — I juggle
options.export_rule: I juggle many things. Help me prioritize and track what's most urgent.

options.label: Depends on the day — sometimes focused, sometimes scattered
options.export_rule: My focus varies. If I seem scattered, help me narrow down. If I'm focused, match my depth.

questions.question: When making decisions, you tend to...
questions.why_this_matters: Determines how AI presents options and recommendations.

options.label: Analyze data first — show me the evidence
options.export_rule: I decide based on data. Present evidence, numbers, and comparisons. Not feelings.

options.label: Trust my gut — give me your recommendation
options.export_rule: Give me your best recommendation with brief reasoning. I'll go with my gut.

options.label: Ask around — consider multiple perspectives
options.export_rule: Present multiple perspectives on decisions. Show me what different people would advise.

options.label: Decide fast, adjust later — momentum matters
options.export_rule: Help me decide fast. Don't over-analyze. Give clear recommendation and let me adjust course.

questions.question: How long can you typically focus without getting distracted?
questions.why_this_matters: Determines how AI structures long explanations and tasks.

options.label: 2+ hours easy — I get in the zone
options.export_rule: I can handle long, detailed responses. Don't break things into tiny pieces unnecessarily.

options.label: 45-60 minutes — then I need a break
options.export_rule: Keep individual explanations under 5 minutes of reading. Break long tasks into 45-min chunks.

options.label: 15-20 minutes — then I need novelty
options.export_rule: Keep responses SHORT. Max 10 lines. Break everything into 15-min actionable chunks. Vary the format.

options.label: Depends — sometimes hours, sometimes 5 minutes
options.export_rule: My focus varies. Default to concise but be ready to go deep if I'm engaged.

questions.question: How do you take breaks during work?
questions.why_this_matters: Affects how AI structures tasks and time estimates.

options.label: Pomodoro style — 25 min work, 5 min break
options.export_rule: I work in pomodoro cycles. Structure tasks in 25-min blocks when possible.

options.label: Long marathons then crash
options.export_rule: I work in long stretches. Don't suggest breaks — I'll stop when I crash.

options.label: Random — when I feel like it
options.export_rule: I take breaks randomly. Don't assume any specific work rhythm.

options.label: What breaks? I just keep going
options.export_rule: I don't take scheduled breaks. If I'm in flow, keep the momentum going.
